methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ERC20 base token reserves held by each MagicLP pool contract (_BASE_TOKEN_ balance)
ERC20 quote token reserves held by each MagicLP pool contract (_QUOTE_TOKEN_ balance)
MagicLP ERC20 LP shares (pool share token minted/burned by MagicLP)
Trading fees retained in-pool (implicit via PMM curve) and maintainer fees paid out to IFeeRateModel.maintainer()
Flash-loanable base/quote token liquidity from MagicLP pools (flashLoan)
Router-held transient assets: WETH minted from received ETH; LP shares temporarily held during removeLiquidity/removeLiquidityETH; ETH held briefly for refunds
LockingMultiRewards stakingToken balances (stakingTokenBalance accounting) and any reward token balances deposited for distribution
Reward token balances held in LockingMultiRewards for pending reward locks and payouts
state_assets: Factory registry state: implementation, maintainerFeeRateModel, pools[base][quote] lists, userPools[creator] lists
FeeRateModel state: maintainer, implementation (fee-rate implementation contract)
LockingMultiRewards user staking state: _balances[user]{unlocked,locked}, _userLocks[user][], lastLockIndex[user], lockedSupply, unlockedSupply, stakingTokenBalance, minLockAmount
LockingMultiRewards user rewards accounting: userRewardPerTokenPaid[user][token], rewards[user][token], _userRewardLock[user]{items[],unlockTime}
Router path/directions encoding for multi-hop swaps (directions bitfield)
Toolkit addressMap/pairCodeHash/cauldron registries (testing/deployment tooling; not intended for production)
privilege_assets: MagicLP implementation owner (solmate Owned.owner() on implementation contract) controls: rescue(), setParameters(), ratioSync() across all clones via onlyImplementationOwner
Factory owner controls: setLpImplementation(), setMaintainerFeeRateModel(), addPool(), removePool() (registry integrity + future pool implementations)
FeeRateModel owner controls: setMaintainer(), setImplementation() (changes fee recipient and fee logic affecting all pools referencing it)
LockingMultiRewards owner controls: addReward(), setMinLockAmount(), recover(), pause(), unpause(), setOperator() (via OperatableV2)
LockingMultiRewards operators (operators mapping) can: stakeFor(), notifyRewardAmount(), processExpiredLocks()
Router has no admin but is a critical integration/entry point for user flows
OperatableV2 owner is central authority for operator set
Pausable authority (LockingMultiRewards owner) can halt staking/locking but withdraw() remains callable
reputation_assets: LP share supply distribution (MagicLP ERC20) affects governance/TVL perception and integrator trust
Maintainer fee recipient address (FeeRateModel.maintainer) and fee logic (FeeRateModel.implementation) affect protocol revenue and perceived fairness
LockingMultiRewards lock positions and pending reward locks (userRewardLock) represent user yield/reputation positions and can affect protocol trust if mis-accounted
Factory pool registry integrity (pools/userPools) affects UI/indexers and perceived legitimacy of pools

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: MagicLP inherits (solady) ERC20 + ReentrancyGuard and (solmate) Owned. Factory inherits (solmate) Owned. FeeRateModel inherits (solmate) Owned. OperatableV2 inherits (solmate) Owned; LockingMultiRewards inherits OperatableV2 and (OpenZeppelin) Pausable. Router is standalone. Toolkit/BaseTest/BaseScript are forge/testing/deployment utilities (not intended for mainnet runtime).
external_dependencies: forge-std (Vm/Test/Script) and forge-deploy (deployment tooling) used in Toolkit/BaseTest/BaseScript/CauldronDeployLib
External callback interface ICallee (used by MagicLP for flash loan + sellShares callbacks)
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: No proxy/UUPS/transparent/beacon patterns detected. MagicLP uses deterministic minimal proxy clones (LibClone) with an init() initializer guarded by _INITIALIZED_. Factory can be considered an 'implementation switcher' by changing the implementation address for future clones only; existing clones remain bound to their implementation immutable stored in each clone via MagicLP.implementation. FeeRateModel supports 'logic swapping' by updating an implementation address that is called in getFeeRate (external view call).
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Ownership-based access control via solmate Owned across MagicLP (implementation owner used for clone admin), Factory, FeeRateModel, and OperatableV2/LockingMultiRewards. LockingMultiRewards adds operator role (operators mapping) with onlyOperators modifier. LockingMultiRewards additionally uses OpenZeppelin Pausable with owner-exposed pause/unpause. MagicLP uses onlyImplementationOwner (msg.sender must equal MagicLP.implementation.owner()) rather than clone-local ownership.

state_changes: _INITIALIZED_ set true to lock implementation instance

rewards accounting for user across rewardTokens (rewards,userRewardPerTokenPaid) and global rewardData updates

initializer_guards: _INITIALIZED_ boolean guard in init(); implementation constructor sets _INITIALIZED_=true
upgradeability_surface.notes: Uses LibClone minimal proxy clones (not an upgradeable proxy standard). Factory can change implementation only for future clones; existing clones are not upgradeable.
Not upgradeable; owner can change MagicLP implementation for new clones and set fee model for new pools.
Not proxy-upgradeable, but has a logic pointer (implementation) that can be updated by owner and affects fee computation for all pools referencing it.
Not upgradeable; privileged operators/owner can mutate reward schedules and process locks.
No upgrade pattern; immutable references to WETH and Factory.
Testing/deployment utility; relies on forge cheatcodes; not intended for production deployment.
